decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the peak temperature.

changes.reason: Doubling the spreader conductivity (within the allowed 2.0x step ratio) will significantly improve lateral heat distribution, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the chip's peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
Improved heat extraction efficiency from the chip to the boundaries
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
If this single change does not satisfy the constraint, subsequent steps may need to target the base_material conductivity or fine-tune geometry within legal bounds.
